description:
## Goal

Introduce refinement records as metadata attached to each canonical.
A refinement is a typer-proven fact: 'at *this* call site, the
narrowing would compile to materially better code.' Records are
created, not yet emitted as CLIF bodies — that's fz-rfn.3.

Eligibility predicate v1: ABI shift only (cheap, crisp, high
payoff). Constant fold + dead clause extend in fz-rfn.5.

## What lands

- New struct in src/ir_typer.rs:
  ```
  Refinement {
      sid: SpecId,
      narrowed_key: Vec<Descr>,
      provenance: HashSet<CallsiteId>,
      eligibility: Eligibility,
      emit: bool,           // populated by emission policy (rfn.3)
  }

  enum Eligibility {
      AbiShift,             // v1
      ConstFold,            // rfn.5
      DeadClause,           // rfn.5
      UserSpec,             // explicit @spec annotation, future
  }
  ```

- `Canonical.refinements: Vec<Refinement>` gets populated by
  push_key:
  - Compute callsite K from args.
  - Compare ArgRepr::from_descr(K[i]) to ArgRepr::from_descr
    (canonical.lub_key[i]) per slot.
  - If any slot has Tagged→Raw* shift OR canonical-Raw* →
    narrower-lit (refinement preserves narrow + adds info):
    eligibility = AbiShift. Append refinement record.
  - Record the (block_id, term position) as CallsiteId in
    provenance.
  - De-dup by narrowed_key: multiple callsites with same key
    contribute provenance to the same refinement record.

- Refinements get their own SpecId from `SpecRegistry::intern`.
  At this stage, codegen still emits canonical only — refinement
  SpecIds exist but trap-stub.

- fz-aiz.6 spec-quality diagnostic adapts: report 'callsite K
  has refinement record but emission is gated' (when rfn.3 lands)
  or 'callsite K has narrowing X with no eligibility trigger.'

## What gets removed

- The current shallow_for_key WIP if any traces remain (none in
  HEAD; check working tree before starting).
- Anything in fz-aiz.6's diagnostic that re-resolves with
  spec_registry as a separate replay — refinement records ARE
  the witness; the diagnostic reads them directly.

## Acceptance

- Refinements registered when narrow callers exist. Verify by
  inspecting the registry post-typing: fib has ≥1 refinement
  for [int, int, int]; lookup has ≥1 refinement (none for
  ast_eval_env since lookup's only narrowing is via cons-list
  walk which doesn't trigger ABI shift unless we extend to
  list-elem-class).
- No CLIF emission changes vs fz-rfn.1 — refinements are records
  only at this stage. Goldens stable.
- spec-quality diagnostic surfaces refinement-record information
  cleanly.

## See also

- fz-rfn.1 — canonical-first (blocking dependency).
- src/ir_codegen.rs:782 — ArgRepr::from_descr (eligibility input).
- fz-aiz.6 — spec-quality diagnostic (gets sharper here).
title: fz-rfn.2: refinement records + ABI-shift eligibility predicate
